zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth de Aton於1276年出生在英國達勒姆達勒姆時，她的父親William de Aton II26歲，母親Isabel de Vere18歲。她和John Conyers至少有1個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1354年在她的家鄉享年78歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Aton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Hatton , with loss of initial H-.
topographic name from Middle English atte toun ‘(dweller) at the settlement’ (Old English æt thǣm tūn(e)).

variants.name: Hatton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Hatton (Cheshire, Derbyshire, Lincolnshire, Middlesex, Shropshire, Staffordshire, Warwickshire), Hatton Hall (Cheshire), or Cold and High Hatton (Shropshire). The placenames derive from Old English hǣth ‘heath, heather’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Heath .
English: variant of Atton with prosthetic H-, a topographic name from Middle English atte toun ‘(dweller) at the settlement’ (Old English æt thǣm tūn(e)). Compare Towne .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Chatáin, a patronymic from a personal name meaning ‘servant of (Saint) Catan’; see McHatton .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth de Aton was born in 1276, in Durham, Durham, England, her father, William de Aton II, was 26 and her mother, Isabel de Vere, was 18. She had at least 1 son and 3 daughters with John Conyers. She died in 1354, in her hometown, at the age of 78.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth de Aton est née en 1276, à Durham, Durham, Angleterre, son père, William de Aton II, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Isabel de Vere, avait 18 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles avec John Conyers. Elle est décédée en 1354, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 78 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth de Aton 1276 in Durham, Durham, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Aton II 26 und ihre Mutter Isabel de Vere 18. Sie hatte mit John Conyers mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1354 im Alter von 78 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Aton è nata il nel 1276 nel Durham, Durham, England, sua padre, William de Aton II, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Isabel de Vere, aveva 18 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie con John Conyers. Morì nel 1354, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 78 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Atonが1276年にイギリスダルハムダルハムで生まれた時，父William de Aton IIは26歳，母Isabel de Vereは18歳であった。彼女はJohn Conyersとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1354年に故郷で78歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Aton(이)가 1276년 영국 뒤람 뒤람에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Aton II(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel de Vere(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 John Conyers(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1354년 그녀의 고향에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Aton nasceu em 1276, em Durham, Durham, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Aton II, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Isabel de Vere, tinha 18 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas com John Conyers. Ela faleceu em 1354, em sua cidade natal, com 78 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth de Aton родилась в 1276 г. в местности дурам, дурам, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Aton II было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel de Vere, 18. es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth de Aton nació en 1276, en Durham, Durham, Inglaterra, su papá, William de Aton II, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Isabel de Vere, tenía 18 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas con John Conyers. Murió en 1354, en su pueblo, a la edad de 78 años.
